site_alerts.name: SQL Injection (Union Based)
site_alerts.description: The 'id' parameter in the URL is vulnerable to SQL Injection. Attackers can retrieve sensitive database information using UNION SELECT statements. Payload used: ' UNION SELECT 1, user, password FROM users--
site_alerts.solution: Use prepared statements (Parameterized Queries) and input validation.

site_alerts.name: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
site_alerts.description: The search bar input is reflected back to the user without proper escaping. An attacker can execute arbitrary JavaScript code in the victim's browser. Payload: <script>alert('XSS')</script>
site_alerts.solution: Implement context-sensitive output encoding and use Content Security Policy (CSP).

site_alerts.name: Broken Access Control (IDOR)
site_alerts.description: The application allows users to access other users' private data by modifying the 'user_id' parameter in the URL. For example, changing /profile?id=10 to /profile?id=11 reveals another user's profile.
site_alerts.solution: Implement proper access control checks on the server side for every request.

site_alerts.name: Command Injection (RCE)
site_alerts.description: The application passes unsafe user supplied data (forms) to a system shell. Using the ping command, an attacker was able to inject 'cat /etc/passwd' to read system files.
site_alerts.solution: Avoid system calls. If necessary, use safer alternatives and strictly validate inputs.

site_alerts.name: Security Misconfiguration - Directory Listing
site_alerts.description: Directory listing is enabled on the /uploads/ directory. Attackers can view and download all files uploaded to the server.
site_alerts.solution: Disable directory browsing in the web server configuration (Apache/Nginx).

site_alerts.name: Sensitive Data Exposure (Credit Card)
site_alerts.description: Credit card numbers are stored in the database without encryption. Additionally, the application transmits data over HTTP instead of HTTPS.
site_alerts.solution: Encrypt sensitive data at rest and ensure all data is transmitted over TLS/SSL.

site_alerts.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
site_alerts.description: The 'url' parameter allows the server to make requests to internal network resources. Access to AWS Metadata (169.254.169.254) was confirmed.
site_alerts.solution: Validate and whitelist allowed domains/IPs for outgoing requests.

site_alerts.name: Outdated Component (Apache 2.2)
site_alerts.description: The server is running an outdated version of Apache (v2.2.15) which has known vulnerabilities.
site_alerts.solution: Upgrade the web server to the latest stable version.

site_alerts.name: Weak Password Policy
site_alerts.description: The application allows users to set simple passwords like '123456'. No complexity requirements are enforced.
site_alerts.solution: Enforce strong password policies (min 8 chars, mixed case, special chars).

site_alerts.name: Missing Anti-CSRF Tokens
site_alerts.description: Important state-changing forms (like password change) do not contain anti-CSRF tokens.
site_alerts.solution: Implement Anti-CSRF tokens for all state-changing operations.
